response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with deep TypeScript/Node.js and DLang experience, but does not meet the core mandatory requirement of 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows — their C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project. Financial domain exposure is thin (about one year at RIDE Capital, an investments startup, in a Node/GraphQL stack rather than capital markets or fixed income systems), and there is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience. The profile is a poor match for a specialized C++ fixed-income consulting role.
strengths: Solid low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang, async I/O at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing
Broad polyglot background with proven ability to pick up new stacks quickly
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, roughly a decade ago, and none on Windows-focused development
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain experience; fintech exposure limited to a startup investment platform
No SQL Server or Oracle experience listed (only MySQL/MongoDB)

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering and performance systems background
